title: Chewy Raises Full-Year Guidance After Strong Q3 Results
text: Online pet retailer Chewy exceeded third-quarter expectations, reporting net sales growth of nearly 5% year-over-year to $2.9 billion. The company achieved profitability during the quarter with operating income of $25.6 million, compared to a $9.7 million loss in the prior year period. The retailer's subscription service proved instrumental to performance. Autoship sales expanded 8.7% year-over-year, reaching $2.3 billion and representing approximately 80% of total net sales. CEO Sumit Singh noted that the program enables high ability and predictability in the business and drives customer stickiness for Chewy. Non-discretionary product categories—including consumables and healthcare offerings—comprised 85% of net sales during the quarter. Chewy raised its full-year guidance, now expecting net sales between $11.79 billion and $11.81 billion, representing 6% annual growth. The company also increased adjusted EBITDA margin guidance to 4.6%-4.8%, up from the previous 4.5%-4.7% range. The company continues expanding its veterinary clinic footprint, currently operating six locations with plans for four to eight additional openings in 2024. These clinics are generating cross-selling opportunities and attracting new customers to the platform.
